content: HDBSCAN on text embeddings: min_cluster_size is the dominant knob (small values ~5-10 typical for template-group exemplars); large noise cluster (-1 label) means min_cluster_size too high or weak embeddings; HDBSCAN produces clusters even without real structure so validate. Critical gotcha: neither scikit-learn HDBSCAN nor hdbscan lib supports cosine metric directly — normalise vectors to unit length and use euclidean (equivalent ranking), or pass a precomputed cosine-distance matrix. Noise points must be handled explicitly (keep as singleton clusters in a triage tool, never drop).
